channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🦠 WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in Congo-Uganda a Global Health Emergency
item.description: The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern. Over 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths have been reported, with confirmed cases spreading to major cities including Kinshasa and Goma. (Source: AP News)

item.title: 💥 Drone Strike Hits UAE Nuclear Plant as Iran Ceasefire Teeters
item.description: A drone strike targeted the United Arab Emirates Barakah nuclear power plant, sparking a fire on its perimeter. The attack comes as diplomatic efforts to extend the fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran face mounting challenges. (Source: AP News)

item.title: 📈 Oil Prices Fall, Wall Street Hits Record as Iran Reopens Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Oil prices dropped sharply and US stocks surged to record highs after Iran announced the Strait of Hormuz was open again for commercial tankers. The S&P 500 jumped 1.2% as markets rallied on hopes for de-escalation. (Source: AP News)

item.title: 🇫🇷 Macron in Kenya for Africa Summit as France Seeks New Continental Partnership
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron kicked off a visit to Kenya for the Africa Forward Summit, showcasing France shift from colonial-era dominance toward partnership with African nations. The summit comes amid Frances withdrawal of troops from West Africa. (Source: AP News)

item.title: 🚢 Russia Strikes Chinese Cargo Ship Bound for Ukraine Before Putin-Xi Summit
item.description: A Russian drone struck a Chinese vessel headed for a Ukrainian port, a move that could anger Beijing one day before Vladimir Putin and Xi Jinping were set to hold a summit. The attack follows Russias interception of over 3,000 Ukrainian drones in the past week. (Source: Independent)

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Economy Slows Sharply as Industrial Output and Retail Sales Disappoint
item.description: China growth lost momentum in April, with industrial output cooling and retail sales sinking to over three-year lows as the second-largest economy grapples with higher energy costs from the Iran war and persistently weak domestic demand. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: 🇰🇪 Kenya Paralyzed by Nationwide Fuel Protests as Iran War Drives Price Surge
item.description: Protests over fuel price hikes erupted in Kenyan towns, with a nationwide public transport strike stranding commuters and forcing many to walk to work. Demonstrators lit bonfires and barricaded roads amid anger over record fuel costs. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: 📉 Global Bond Rout Deepens as Iran War Stokes Worldwide Inflation Fears
item.description: Government bonds from Tokyo to New York extended losses as rising energy prices from the Iran conflict fanned inflation fears and stoked investor wagers on rate hikes from central banks around the world. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo to Release First Encyclical on Artificial Intelligence May 25
item.description: Pope Leo will release his first in-depth document on May 25, expected to address the rise of artificial intelligence and challenges to workers rights. The encyclical Magnifica Humanitas will be launched with Anthropic co-founder Christopher Olah. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: 🚢 Hantavirus-Hit Cruise Ship Docks in Netherlands for Disinfection After Deadly Outbreak
item.description: A luxury polar explorer ship arrived at Rotterdam port where authorities quarantined 27 remaining crew and planned to cremate a German passenger who died. The MV Hondius had been at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak during its seven-week voyage. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Forms New Body to Manage Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran's top security council announced the formation of a new authority to manage the strategic Strait of Hormuz, the vital shipping chokepoint that has been closed during the conflict. (Source: Channel News Asia)

item.title: 🛢️ IEA Warns Commercial Oil Inventories Depleting Rapidly
item.description: IEA chief Fatih Birol said commercial oil inventories are depleting rapidly with only weeks worth left due to the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz closure to shipping. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: 🇨🇺 Cuba Denies Seeking Russian Drones, Accuses US of Building Case for Intervention
item.description: Havana's foreign minister rejected a report that Cuba acquired attack drones from Russia, calling it a "fraudulent case" to justify US aggression. (Source: BBC)

item.title: 🎤 Bulgaria Wins Eurovision 2026, Israel Takes Second Place
item.description: Bulgaria's Dara won Eurovision 2026 with her song "Bangaranga," while Israel finished second with 343 points in a competition held under the shadow of regional conflict. (Source: BBC)

item.title: 🇺🇦💥 Ukraine launches large-scale drone strikes on Russia, killing 4 and wounding dozens
item.description: A major Ukrainian drone offensive targeted multiple locations inside Russia, resulting in casualties and demonstrating Kyiv's continued capability to strike deep into enemy territory. (Source: AP News)

item.title: 📉🛢️ Stocks fall and oil rises after Trump warns Iran that clock is ticking
item.description: Financial markets reacted to escalating Iran tensions, with equities declining and crude prices climbing as the US president issued warnings about the Iranian situation. (Source: AP News)

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Built Secret Military Bases in Iraq Before Iran War
item.description: Israel spent over a year preparing covert military sites in western Iraq before launching operations against Iran, according to New York Times reporting. Iraqi officials later confirmed the existence of a second base. (Source: New York Times)

item.title: 🚢 Israeli Navy Intercepts Gaza-Bound Flotilla in International Waters
item.description: Israeli naval forces boarded multiple civilian vessels from the Global Sumud Flotilla carrying humanitarian aid activists, detaining participants and diverting ships to Ashdod port in enforcement of the Gaza maritime blockade. (Source: Daily Sabah)

item.title: ☢️ Belarus Begins Nuclear Weapons Training Exercise With Russia
item.description: Under the leadership of the Belarusian Chief of General Staff, military units began training for combat use of nuclear weapons and nuclear support, working with Russian forces on delivery and preparation of nuclear munitions. (Source: Charter97)

item.title: 🇷🇺🇺🇦 Putin Agrees to NATO-Style Security Guarantees for Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin agreed at his summit with Trump that the US and European allies could offer Ukraine Article 5-like security protections as part of a potential peace deal. Trump envoy Steve Witkoff called it game-changing and a major shift in Russia's position. (Source: AP News)

item.title: 📰 Major Media Outlets Demand Israel Allow Independent Journalists Into Gaza
item.description: Leaders of major media organizations are urging Israel to lift a ban preventing foreign journalists from independently entering Gaza, citing press freedom and public's right to know. (Source: AP News)

item.title: 🇦🇺 Australian Workers to Strike at Inpex LNG Facility Amid Global Energy Crisis
item.description: A union in Australia announced workers will strike at Inpex's Ichthys LNG facility from May 27, a move that could worsen already tight global energy supplies as Middle East tensions continue. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: 🇨🇳 Chinese scientists land on disputed Sandy Cay reef as Philippines tensions escalate
item.description: Beijing researchers landed on the uninhabited South China Sea sandbank over the weekend in the latest assertion of sovereignty claims, amid ongoing territorial disputes with Manila. (Source: SCMP)

item.title: 🇦🇹 Austrian jets intercept US military aircraft two days in a row amid airspace tensions
item.description: Austria scrambled fighter jets to intercept US military planes for the second consecutive day, highlighting growing friction over military flight operations in European airspace. (Source: Defense News)

item.title: ✈️ Israels F-35s to get external fuel tanks after extensive Iran combat operations
item.description: Fresh off combat operations against Iran, Israel is boosting the F-35I Adirs endurance with external fuel tanks to reduce dependence on tanker aircraft for long-range missions. (Source: The War Zone)
